title: Handle Salesforce IP-Restricted OAuth Error with User-Friendly Error Page and Notifications
story: As a Salesforce administrator whose org enforces IP restrictions, I want to see a clear error message explaining why I cannot authenticate with DataTools Pro so that I can resolve the IP restriction issue in my Salesforce settings without contacting support.
description:
When a Salesforce org has IP restrictions configured on user profiles and the Connected App uses 'Enforce IP Restrictions', users attempting to authenticate from a non-whitelisted IP receive a generic 400 Bad Request error from the Salesforce OAuth endpoint.

## Problem
The previous behavior showed a generic error page with no actionable information. Since the error occurs during the OAuth callback, Salesforce does not identify the specific user — only a "Guest User" error is returned.

## Solution
1. Detect the IP-restricted error from the Salesforce OAuth callback response
2. Display a specific, user-friendly error page explaining the IP restriction issue
3. Send notification emails to administrators when this error occurs (user shown as "Guest User" since identity is unknown)
4. Create a tutorial page explaining how to resolve the IP restriction: whitelist DataTools Pro IP ranges in the Salesforce profile's Login IP Ranges

## Tutorial
Published at: https://datatoolspro.com/tutorials/how-to-resolve-ip-restricted-error-and-grant-access-to-our-app/
acceptance_criteria: IP-restricted OAuth error is detected and handled specifically
User sees a clear error page explaining the IP restriction issue
Notification email is sent to administrators on IP-restricted authentication failure
Notification email identifies the user as 'Guest User' when identity cannot be determined
Tutorial page is published explaining how to resolve IP restrictions in Salesforce
Error page links to or references the tutorial for self-service resolution
ux_changes: Generic error page replaced with a specific IP-restricted error page that explains the issue and provides resolution steps. Links to a tutorial for self-service resolution.
middleware_changes: OAuth callback error handling now detects IP-restricted errors from Salesforce and routes to the specific error page. Notification email system triggers on IP-restricted authentication failures.
implementation_notes: The error occurs during Salesforce OAuth callback when a user's profile has Login IP Ranges configured and the Connected App enforces IP restrictions. Since the error is pre-authentication, Salesforce does not return user identity — only an error message. The notification email uses 'Guest User' as a placeholder.
comment_summary: Waqar explained the IP restriction enforcement setup and proposed showing the exact error message to customers instead of a generic screen. He implemented a specific error page, set up admin notification emails, and published a tutorial on resolving IP restrictions in Salesforce.
